0.q: the level of total carotenoid was elevated up to 2.7-fold (38.1 mg g-1DW) compared to the non-transgenic control; transcript levels for most of carotenogenesis-related genes were elevated in transgenic tuber

IbOr-R96H transgenic calli accumulated dramatically higher levels of total carotenoids... The total carotenoid content in IbOr-R96H transgenic calli was 23.8-fold higher than the level in the Ym callus.
the sweetpotato Orange gene (IbOr) is involved in increasing carotenoid accumulation and enhancing resistance to multiple abiotic stresses
overexpressing the sweet potato Or (IbOr) gene also increased the carotenoid contents in transgenic potato tubers and sweet potato storage roots

1.q: The activities of superoxide dismutase (SOD) ... were significantly enhanced in the transgenic plants. H2O2 was also found to be significantly less accumulated in the transgenic plants.

The activities of superoxide dismutase (SOD) and photosynthesis were significantly enhanced in the transgenic plants. H2O2 was also found to be significantly less accumulated in the transgenic plants.

4.q: overexpression of IbZFP1 up-regulated the genes involved in ... ROS scavenging ... the activities of ... superoxide dismutase, catalase, ascorbate peroxidase, and peroxidase were also increased

6.q: overexpression of IbMIPS1 up-regulated the genes involved in inositol biosynthesis, phosphatidylinositol (PI) and ABA signalling pathways under salt, drought and stem nematode stresses.

8.q: The ROS scavenging genes encoding SOD, glutathione peroxidase (GPX), catalase (CAT), ascorbate peroxidase (APX) and dehydroascorbate reductase (DHAR) were found to show a systematic upregulation in the transgenic plants under salt and drought stresses

9.q: Overexpression of IbMYB330 up-regulated genes involved in the reactive oxygen species (ROS) scavenging system, thereby improving drought and salt resistance in transgenic tobacco.

11.q: the expression levels of IbAPX, IbPOD, and IbCAT were significantly downregulated in RNAi plants, which suggests that IbCAR1 can regulate ROS-scavenging pathways

12.q: the CAT activity, POD activity, proline content, and protein content in transgenic tobacco had increased, while MDA content had decreased

13.q: Overexpression of IbNAC087 increased salt and drought tolerance by increasing jasmonic acid (JA) accumulation and activating reactive oxygen species (ROS) scavenging.

14.q: Overexpression of IbNAC087 increased salt and drought tolerance by increasing jasmonic acid (JA) accumulation and activating reactive oxygen species (ROS) scavenging.

15.q: JA‐rich IbNAC087‐OE plants exhibited more stomatal closure than wild‐type (WT) and IbNAC087‐Ri plants under NaCl, polyethylene glycol, and methyl jasmonate treatments.

17.q: Overexpression of IbBBX24, IbTOE3 and IbPRX17 significantly increased salt and drought stress tolerance by promoting ROS scavenging.

19.q: Overexpression of IbCHR10 enhanced salt tolerance, evidenced by improved growth, reduced oxidative damage, increased antioxidant enzyme activity, enhanced osmotic adjustment, and elevated ABA and JA accumulation under salt stress.

21.q: High performance liquid chromatography (HPLC) analysis showed a significant increase of total carotenoids in transgenic plants.

23.q: several genes related to the ABA signaling pathway, proline biosynthesis, and the reactive oxygen species (ROS)-scavenging system, were significantly up-regulated in transgenic lines.

24.q: The systemic up-regulation of reactive oxygen species (ROS) scavenging genes was found in the transgenic plants under salt stress.

25.q: IbGAD4 overexpression induced GABA pathway gene expression and GABA accumulation, upregulated the Salt Overly Sensitive (SOS) pathway and enhanced salt tolerance.

28.q: IbHAK11 positively regulates ROS scavenging to maintain ROS homeostasis and enhance resistance to high salinity.

32.q: Under normal culture conditions, lignin contents increased in IbLEA14-overexpressing calli because of the increased expression of a variety of monolignol biosynthesis-related genes.

79.q: the overexpression of KIbB1 enhanced K+ uptake, but the translocation from root to shoot was not affected under salt stress. This demonstrates that KIbB1 acted as a positive regulator in high-salinity stress resistance through regulating Na+ and K+ uptake to maintain K+/Na+ homeostasis.
